## 2026 FIFA World Cup Group H — Matchday 1 & 2 Research Report

### Group H Teams
- **Spain**
- **Cape Verde** (first-ever World Cup appearance)
- **Saudi Arabia**
- **Uruguay**

---

### Matchday 1 Results (June 15, 2026)

**Match 1: Spain 0 – 0 Cape Verde**
- **Date:** June 15, 2026
- **Result:** Goalless draw
- Cape Verde, in their historic first-ever World Cup match, held Spain to a 0-0 draw — a remarkable result for the tournament debutants.
- Source: FOX Sports

**Match 2: Saudi Arabia 1 – 1 Uruguay**
- **Date:** June 15, 2026
- **Venue:** Miami
- **Scorers:** Abdulelah Al Amri (Saudi Arabia, 1st half); Maxi Araujo (Uruguay, 80')
- Saudi Arabia took a first-half lead before Uruguay equalized late through Araujo.
- Sources: FOX Sports, Yahoo Sports

### Group H Standings After Matchday 1

| Pos | Team | P | W | D | L | GF | GA | GD | Pts |
|-----|------|---|---|---|---|----|----|-----|-----|
| 1 | Cape Verde | 1 | 0 | 1 | 0 | 0 | 0 | 0 | **1** |
| 2 | Saudi Arabia | 1 | 0 | 1 | 0 | 1 | 1 | 0 | **1** |
| 3 | Spain | 1 | 0 | 1 | 0 | 0 | 0 | 0 | **1** |
| 4 | Uruguay | 1 | 0 | 1 | 0 | 1 | 1 | 0 | **1** |

All four teams level on 1 point after both matches ended in draws.

---

### Matchday 2 Status

**Matchday 2 results are NOT yet available.** The Matchday 2 fixtures (Spain vs Saudi Arabia and Cape Verde vs Uruguay) are scheduled for approximately June 20–21, 2026 — which has not yet occurred as of today (June 16, 2026). No scores can be reported for these matches.

---

### Key Context
- **Cape Verde** qualified for the World Cup for the first time under the expanded 48-team format.
- **Saudi Arabia** entered with recent World Cup pedigree, notably their famous 2–1 upset of Argentina at the 2022 World Cup.
- June 15 was described as a "wild day" of World Cup action, also featuring Belgium 1–1 Egypt and Iran 2–2 New Zealand in Group G.
